payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because it is the most recent (active) version of that information. The earlier mem_old entry for Sam was filtered out because it was marked as superseded by the newer memory, so only the updated claim was used.
payload.conflict_classification: Version conflict / supersession conflict (newer memory overrides older memory for the same entity and claim).
payload.decision: Prefer mem_new (Sam at Goldman) over mem_old because mem_old is superseded by a more recent/updated record.

entity_resolution.reason: Sam in mem_new and mem_old is treated as the same entity; the system then compares versions and keeps the newer record while discarding the superseded one.

payload.intent: Explain why recall returned the newer memory for Sam from Goldman and excluded the older one because the older one was superseded.

memory_cards.statement: Recall selected the newer memory for Sam associated with Goldman, returning it as the active result.
Recall filtered out the older memory for Sam because it was superseded by a newer update.

details: Selected mem_new as the preferred result due to newer/active status.
Filtered mem_old because it was identified as superseded.

repair_options: If both memories should be retained, add a disambiguation note so they are treated as complementary facts rather than superseding versions.
If mem_old was incorrectly marked superseded, update the memory metadata/timestamps or supersession rules for that entry.
